title: What 'Put It To Death' Actually Means For A Man Of God (It's Not Willpower)
caption:
You've been calling it a struggle. Scripture calls it something else.

Colossians and Romans don't say manage your sin, or work on it, or set a boundary with it. They say put it to death. The porn, the laziness, the cowardice, the version of you that scrolls instead of prays, all of it named plainly, not softened into a code word you can live with.

Grit gets you a few clean days. Killing sin looks more ordinary than that: name it out loud, cut off what feeds it, ask God to do in you what gritted teeth can't, then say it to one brother who already knows your mess.

"If by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the flesh, you will live." — Romans 8:13

You don't manage what's killing you. You kill it, with help you didn't manufacture yourself.

Save this for the next time you catch yourself saying "I'm working on it."

#faith #christianmen #discipline #godlymen #manofgod #putittodeath #romans8 #scripture #christianity #selfcontrol
theme: He kills what's killing him — Scripture says put sin to death, not manage it.
